title: piquant spicy norteño chicken torta de jugo
description: this umami-filled dish combines the richness of chicken with the spicy kick of norteño peppers, all wrapped up in a weeknight-friendly torta. enjoy with a glass of horchata!

ingredients.name: fines herbes
ingredients.notes: chopped (such as parsley, chives, and cilantro)

instructions: heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
reduce the heat to medium and add the sliced norteño peppers to the skillet.
cook the peppers for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
add the sliced shallots and minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until the shallots are translucent.
add the cumin, chili powder, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
return the chicken to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
reduce the heat to low and simmer the chicken and pepper mixture for 5-7 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
warm 4 tortas on a baking sheet in the oven for 2-3 minutes.
assemble the tortas by placing a portion of the chicken and pepper mixture on each torta, followed by a sprinkle of chopped fines herbes.
serve immediately, with a glass of horchata on the side.
